name: California BBQ Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, well-seasoned cast-iron Dutch oven with a cover, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
2. Add the minced onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for an additional minute to release its flavors.
4. Pour in the water, brown sugar, orange juice, apple cider vinegar, light soy sauce, liquid barbecue smoke flavoring, cayenne pepper, and black pepper.
5. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat slightly so steam can escape through the lid ajar.
6. Simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
7. Salt the meat (e.g., chicken ribs) as desired before adding it to the sauce. Do not marinate the meat in the sauce; instead, cook it slowly simmering until tender.
